section.name: IANA Considerations
section.t: This document requests IANA registration only of the media types in . AIP uses existing registered mechanisms for HTTP authorization and discovery: the Bearer and DPoP authentication schemes, OAuth Authorization Server Metadata, and OAuth Protected Resource Metadata. The did:aip DID method, AIP scope identifiers, AID namespaces, grant tier values, and AIP error code values are not IANA registries in this version of the specification; their change control is described in the subsections below.

section.name: Non-IANA DID Method Registry
section.t: The W3C DID Method Registry is not an IANA registry. This document requests no IANA action for the did:aip DID method. If the did:aip method is submitted to the W3C DID Method Registry, the following registration information applies.

tr.td: The principal or deployer that controls the registered Agent Identity Object and the current private key corresponding to the AID public key.
did:aip:<namespace>:<32-lowercase-hex-agent-id>; the complete ABNF is defined in .
Create is performed through POST /v1/agents using a Registration Envelope validated by .
Read is performed through DID resolution against the authoritative Registry and the GET /v1/agents/{aid} and public-key endpoints defined in .

The DID identifier is immutable. The only protocol-defined update operation is key rotation through PUT /v1/agents/{aid}, with previous-key authorization as defined in or fallback-key authorization as defined in .
Deactivate is represented by accepted Revocation Objects and DID Document deactivation metadata as defined in and .

AIDs are derived from Ed25519 public key material, key rotation is versioned and previous-key signed, historical keys are retained for validation, and revocation is checked according to the Tier rules in this specification.
AID resolution exposes agent metadata necessary for validation. Registries MUST follow the token payload non-retention and privacy-preserving audit requirements in and .

section.name: Existing HTTP and Discovery Registrations
section.t: AIP Credential Tokens and Step Execution Tokens are presented using the registered Bearer HTTP authentication scheme for requests that do not require proof-of-possession, and using the registered DPoP HTTP authentication scheme for DPoP-bound requests. AIP Registries that act as OAuth Authorization Servers publish authorization-server metadata using the registered oauth-authorization-server well-known URI suffix defined by . AIP-protected gateways publish protected resource metadata using the registered oauth-protected-resource well-known URI suffix defined by .

This document does not define a new HTTP authentication scheme and does not define a new Well-Known URI. Registry Metadata is published at the ordinary Registry API endpoint /v1/registry-metadata relative to the Registry's registry_id origin.

section.name: AIP Scope Identifiers
section.t: AIP scope identifiers are OAuth scope-token compatible dot-notation strings defined by the applicable AIP Catalog Snapshot. This document does not define a URN namespace for AIP scope values.
Concrete AIP scope registrations are maintained in the external AIP Catalog for the applicable draft snapshot. This document does not request IANA maintenance of individual AIP scope values.

section.name: AID Namespace Catalog
section.t: Concrete did:aip namespace registrations are maintained in the external AIP Catalog for the applicable draft snapshot. IANA is not requested to maintain individual AIP namespace values in this document.

section.name: AIP Grant Tier Values
section.t: Grant tier values are protocol values defined by this specification, not an IANA registry. This document requests no IANA action for grant tier values. Future changes to these values require a specification update that defines the new value, its registration and authorization behavior, and its conformance impact.

section.name: AIP Error Codes
section.t: AIP error codes are protocol values defined by this specification, not an IANA registry. This document requests no IANA action for AIP error code values. Future changes to these values require a specification update that defines the code, the default HTTP status, the applicable endpoint or validation step, and the corresponding error response requirements.

dl.dd: Token malformed, invalid signature, or invalid claims.
Token exp is in the past.
Token jti seen before within validity window.
Request syntax, query parameter, pagination cursor, or endpoint-specific request parameter is invalid.
X-AIP-Version or token aip_version is missing, unsupported, or mutually inconsistent.
DPoP proof is required for the request but the DPoP header is absent. Malformed or invalid DPoP proofs return invalid_token.
The AID has been revoked.
Operation not within granted scopes.
delegation_depth mismatch or exceeds max_delegation_depth.
Principal Token in aip_chain expired.
Structural error in delegation chain.
Capability Manifest unavailable, signature failed, or AIP Scope Catalog constraint validation failed.
Capability Manifest expires_at passed.
Approval Envelope malformed, signature failed, dependency invalid, or value/hash mismatch.
The Approval Envelope was not approved before approval_window_expires_at.
Approval Envelope ID not found.
Approval Envelope state changed before the requested approval or rejection transition could be committed.
triggered_by step not yet completed.
Step already claimed by another actor.
Step Execution Token presented before the Registry has accepted a claim for that step.
Presented action_parameters hash does not match stored action_hash.
Step Execution Token verification against Registry failed.
An Approval Envelope compensation step failed and the envelope is in terminal failed state requiring out-of-band remediation.
The referenced Approval Envelope or step was cancelled because its parent Engagement Object was terminated. The operation cannot proceed.
AIP-GRANT request_expires_at passed.
AIP-GRANT grant_request_id seen before.

GrantRequest malformed, expired, missing from a G3 authorization request, signature failed, inconsistent with OAuth request binding, or cannot be resolved to trusted catalog scope display metadata for consent.
Principal declined the grant.
GrantResponse nonce does not match.
Registered grant_tier is absent, unrecognised, or below the Grant Tier required for the operation's security Tier.
Registration Envelope malformed or failed validation, including missing or invalid grant_tier.
The submitted AID or public key is already associated with a registered, non-revoked AID.
AID not registered in any accessible Registry.
Agent Identity key rotation version is stale, skips a version, or conflicts with a committed rotation.
Revocation Object is malformed, uses a reserved externally submitted reason, has an invalid timestamp, or has an invalid or unverifiable signature.
Revocation issuer is not authorized for the target or revocation type.
Revocation identifier was previously accepted with different object content.
Registry or a required DID resolver could not be reached or timed out.
Rate limit for this operation exceeded; see Section 19.
Tier 3 operation without mTLS.

Requested scope is unknown, reserved, removed, retired (including bare spawn_agents), conflicting with the synced AIP Scope Catalog, or outside an explicitly documented local/private extension policy.
Principal DID method is not permitted for the operation Tier; Tier 2 and Tier 3 require did:web.
G3 identity proofing is absent or does not satisfy requested acr_values.
The enterprise IdP denied the token exchange request due to Conditional Access, ABAC, or equivalent enterprise policy.
The AIP Registry's enterprise IdP client credentials are invalid or expired.
The enterprise IdP rejected the secondary token exchange grant.
aip_originator_aid is absent in an A2A delegated context, malformed, self-referential, or not bound to the validated delegation chain.
Endorsement Object validation failed, including invalid signature or out-of-range score.
A gateway OAuth Protected Resource Metadata document is internally inconsistent or violates AIP gateway metadata constraints.
Tier 3 Credential Token does not contain the required aip_principal_assertion claim.
aip_principal_assertion is malformed, has an untrusted issuer, or fails enterprise IdP signature verification.
aip_principal_assertion.sub does not match the root Principal Token principal identifier in aip_chain[0].
G1 grant_id not found or expired.
G1 grant_id does not match deployer.
G3 authorization request missing PKCE or not using code_challenge_method S256.
Registry does not match principal DID-Document-declared Registry.
Overlay violates CO-1 attenuation rule or AIP Scope Catalog constraint validation.
Overlay issuer uses did:key.
Overlay version not strictly increasing.
Overlay signature verification failure.
Engagement has been terminated or completed.
Engagement is currently suspended.
Agent removed from engagement.
Required approval gate not yet approved.
Engagement ID not found.
Missing required Engagement Object countersignature.
Engagement Object signature missing, malformed, bound to the wrong DID, or failed verification.
Attempt to modify change log entry.
Out-of-sequence change log append.
RPNP subscription request missing DPoP, using an invalid DPoP proof, or failing to bind subscriber_did to the DPoP key or Authorization credential.
scope_filter: "all" rejected by Registry policy.
Webhook URI not HTTPS.
RPNP subscription limit reached.
Token exchange resource not registered.

section.name: Media Types
section.t: IANA is requested to register the following media types in the "Media Types" registry using the registration procedure defined by .

tr.td: AIP Certificate Revocation List (signed JSON format)

section.t: The typ header value for AIP Credential Tokens is AIP+JWT per RFC 7515. The typ header value for AIP Step Execution Tokens is AIP-SET+JWT.

dl.dd: application
aip+jwt
N/A
N/A
7bit; values use compact JWT/JWS serialization.
See and the security considerations of and . AIP Credential Tokens are authorization credentials and can be bearer credentials unless bound to proof-of-possession material.
Implementations need the AIP Credential Token profile and validation rules in and .
This document.
dl.dt: Applications that use this media type:
dl.dd: AIP agents, Registries, relying parties, and OAuth authorization servers that exchange or validate AIP Credential Tokens.
The syntax and semantics of fragment identifiers are not defined for this media type.
Magic number(s): N/A; file extension(s): N/A; Macintosh file type code(s): N/A.
dl.dt: Person and email address to contact for further information:
dl.dd: Paras Singla <paras.singla@inviscel.com>
COMMON
None
Paras Singla <paras.singla@inviscel.com>
IETF
application
aip-set+jwt
N/A
N/A
7bit; values use compact JWT/JWS serialization.

See and the security considerations of and . AIP Step Execution Tokens authorize individual approval-envelope steps and require audience, actor, step, status, expiry, and proof-of-possession validation before use.
Implementations need the Step Execution Token validation profile in .
This document.
dl.dt: Applications that use this media type:
dl.dd: AIP Registries, agents, and relying parties that claim or execute approval-envelope steps.
The syntax and semantics of fragment identifiers are not defined for this media type.
Magic number(s): N/A; file extension(s): N/A; Macintosh file type code(s): N/A.
dl.dt: Person and email address to contact for further information:
dl.dd: Paras Singla <paras.singla@inviscel.com>
COMMON
None
Paras Singla <paras.singla@inviscel.com>
IETF
application
aip-crl+json
N/A
N/A
8bit; values are JSON encoded using UTF-8.
See and . AIP CRL documents carry revocation status and require signature, freshness, issuer, and pagination validation before relying parties use them for authorization decisions.
Implementations need the CRL structure and revocation-status validation rules in .
This document.
dl.dt: Applications that use this media type:
dl.dd: AIP Registries and relying parties that publish, fetch, cache, or verify revocation lists.
The syntax and semantics of fragment identifiers are not defined for this media type.
Magic number(s): N/A; file extension(s): N/A; Macintosh file type code(s): N/A.
dl.dt: Person and email address to contact for further information:
dl.dd: Paras Singla <paras.singla@inviscel.com>
COMMON
None
Paras Singla <paras.singla@inviscel.com>
IETF
